subject: Statistics
title: Scatter Plots and Trends
description: A quick win: understand Scatter Plots and Trends.

hook.text: Plot study hours against exam scores. A cloud of dots emerges — and a trend hides inside.

buildup.text: Scatter plots show the relationship between two variables. Each dot is one observation.

discovery.text: Upward trends mean positive correlation. Downward means negative. No pattern means no relationship.

twist.text: Patterns can be misleading. A curve, a cluster, or an outlier can hide the true story.

climax.text: Always plot your data before fitting a line. Anscombe's quartet proved that numbers alone can deceive.

punchline.text: Before you calculate, visualize. The plot tells first.

quiz.question: Why should you create a scatter plot before running regression?
options: It reveals patterns, outliers, and curves that summary statistics can miss
It automatically fits the regression line
It removes outliers from the data
